fixes_applied: Added sustainability evidence diagnostics to service selection metadata.
Added per-team eligible beat and suppression audit fields.
Filtered positive optionality-only candidates out of public sustainability selection.
Polished sustainability wording around named arms, narrow routes, and forward constraints.
initial_findings: Before this pass, the real audit selected depth_constraint 17, route_change 12, coverage_pressure 1, sustainability_question 0, neutral 0.
Sustainability rarity needed evidence-level explanation instead of forced distribution.

strongest_story_outputs.headline: The route has changed, now running through Paul Sewald, Kevin Ginkel, and Taylor Clarke.

sections.baseline: The comparison point is the previous route: Drey Jameson, Philip Abner, and Kade Strowd. The current route retained 0 arms from that baseline.
sections.cause: The added arms are Paul Sewald, Kevin Ginkel, and Taylor Clarke. The arms moving out of that route are Drey Jameson, Philip Abner, and Kade Strowd.
sections.constraint: If the next game tightens, the route points back through Paul Sewald, Kevin Ginkel, and Taylor Clarke.
sections.headline: The route has changed, now running through Paul Sewald, Kevin Ginkel, and Taylor Clarke.
sections.observation: The important-outs route now runs through Paul Sewald, Kevin Ginkel, and Taylor Clarke. That is a 3-spot change from the prior route.

strongest_story_outputs.headline: Athletics' bullpen has fewer practical paths than the active list suggests

sections.baseline: The active list still shows 8 bullpen arms.
sections.cause: The inactive group includes Braden Nett, Brady Basso, Brooks Kriske, and Chen Zhong-Ao Zhuang. The depth loss includes 4 IL arms. It also includes 7 non-IL inactive arms.
sections.constraint: If the roster stays this thin, the practical path remains smaller than the active list suggests. The current route has 3 practical close-game paths. The available route is narrow.
sections.headline: Athletics' bullpen has fewer practical paths than the active list suggests
sections.observation: There are 11 bullpen arms outside the active route. That leaves the practical path heavy.

worst_story_outputs.headline: Chicago White Sox' rotation is handing more of the game to the bullpen

sections.baseline: The comparison point is 2.8 starter innings over the full 14-day window. The current seven-day handoff is 2.4 innings.
sections.cause: The starters are not covering as many innings as the recent baseline. Shorter starts are pushing 5.7 bullpen innings per game into the relief group. The rotation has been handing the game to the bullpen earlier.
sections.constraint: If short starts continue, the bullpen has fewer clean ways to spread the innings.
sections.headline: Chicago White Sox' rotation is handing more of the game to the bullpen
sections.observation: The starters are averaging 2.4 innings over the last week. That recent starter length is down 0.4 innings from the two-week mark. The bullpen has entered before the sixth in 81.8% of recent games.
